content.full_text:
DAVID R. CHAPMAN
CITY ATTORNEY

JEFFERSON C. DALY
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

MARK A. WAGGONER
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY/LITIGATION

MARTIN A. GROVER
SENIOR DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

CAROLYN A. ARNOLD
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

CITY OF ESCONDIDO

CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE
CIVIC CENTER PLAZA
201 North Broadway, Escondido
California 92025
(619) 741-4608
FAX (619) 741-7541

March 25, 1992

California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Request for Advice

I am writing this letter pursuant to Government Code §83114 to request an opinion on whether a particular legal entity is subject to the Political Reform Act.

In the early 1980's local groups advocated construction of facilities to provide a conference and meeting center for residents and visitors, and cultural facilities for the performing and fine arts. The Escondido Civic Center Master Plan was approved by the City Council in cooperation with the Civic Center Committee, which was composed of Escondido citizens. In a brochure distributed in 1985, it was noted that public participation was a key ingredient in the approval process. Indeed, the City Council placed the decision whether or not the Civic Center Project should go forward on the ballot for a vote by the people in June of 1985, where it was approved.

The Center for the Arts Escondido is a California nonprofit corporation incorporated on October 17, 1989. The Center for the Arts, Escondido incorporated for the purpose of operating and managing the arts, theatre, and conference center approved in the City of Escondido by the voters in 1985. The arts, theatre, and conference center are presently under construction and completion is expected during 1993-1994. The projected cost of the facilities is $73.4 million. These monies have come from tax increment revenue generated by the Escondido Redevelopment Area. The Escondido Community Development Commission is overseeing construction of the facilities.

The Center for the Arts Escondido is governed by a Board of Trustees. The Articles and Bylaws of the Center for the Arts provide for a Board consisting of twenty-one (21) persons. Twelve (12) of the Trustees are to be appointed by the Escondido City Council, and the remaining nine (9) Trustees are to be selected by the other members of the Board of Trustees. Two Escondido City Council members sit as Trustees. At the direction of the Community Development

JERRY C. HARMON, MAYOR     CARLA DeDOMINICIS, MAYOR PRO TEM     KRIS MURPHY     SID HOLLINS     RICHARD A. FOSTER

Printed on Recycled Paper

March 25, 1992
Page 2

Commission, the Commission's general counsel prepared documents and
handled incorporation. Copies of the Bylaws and Articles of Incorporation are
attached.

Operating expenses of the Center for the Arts Escondido are funded primarily
by private donations at the present time, with event-generated revenue expected
when construction of the Center is complete. In addition, the City of Escondido
placed $2.29 million in trust in 1989, with the direction that the principal remain
in trust and income be used for operating expenses. At present, the average
interest income is $150,000.00. The City has also budgeted an amount not to
exceed $500,000 from the City's general fund toward the Center's operating costs
each year.

The annual operating budget of The Center will be approximately $4,000,000 in
the next two or three years, making the City's contribution less than one-eighth
of the total.

As a result of a recent amendment to §6252(b) of the California Government
Code (Stats 1991, Chapter 181), The Center is not subject to the Public Records
Act.

The Center for the Arts nonprofit corporation is not only in charge of operating
and managing the arts, theatre, and conference center, but also has replaced the
Felicita Foundation, a completely private entity providing cultural and arts
activities in the city for a number of years.

It has been the objective of the City Council of the City of Escondido that the
Center not be run on an ongoing basis by the City. Indeed, the City Council
desires to have a separate Board and substantial private interests involved in the
operations of the Center as much as possible.

The question on which your assistance is requested is whether or not the
Trustees are subject to the requirements of the Political Reform Act of 1974,
Government Code §8100-91015.

Please let me know if you would like to discuss this issue further. As well, I can
provide any particular facts you may find necessary to your consideration of this
matter.

Very truly yours,

JEFFREY R. EPP
Assistant City Attorney

sections.question: The question on which your assistance is requested is whether or not the Trustees are subject to the requirements of the Political Reform Act of 1974, Government Code §81006-91015.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Trustees of the Center for the Arts Escondido, a nonprofit corporation incorporated to operate arts and conference facilities approved by voters, are subject to the Political Reform Act of 1974.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections but contains a clear legal question at the end. Some text is partially illegible due to scan quality. No FPPC advice/conclusion is present in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The question on which your assistance is requested is whether or not the Trustees are subject to the requirements of the Political Reform Act of 1974, Government Code §81006-91015.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Escondido requests FPPC advice on whether Trustees of the Center for the Arts Escondido nonprofit corporation are subject to Political Reform Act requirements. The Center operates facilities funded primarily by private donations and public trust funds, with a Board containing appointed city council members and independently selected trustees.
